quizQuestion: Which body system does cardi/o belong to?
explanation: Cardi/o (heart) belongs to the cardiovascular system. Other cardiovascular roots include arteri/o (artery), phleb/o (vein), angi/o (vessel), and ven/o (vein).
statement: Cardi/o belongs to the cardiovascular system.
wowFactor: The cardiovascular system has more root words than almost any other system — cardi/o, angi/o, arteri/o, phleb/o, ven/o, and hem/o all belong here!

variants.question: Name a combining form from the cardiovascular system.
A cardiologist specializes in which body system?

visualDescription: Root CARDI/O with an arrow pointing to a cardiovascular system diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does arteri/o belong to?
explanation: Arteri/o (artery) belongs to the cardiovascular system. Other cardiovascular roots include cardi/o (heart), phleb/o (vein), angi/o (vessel), and aort/o (aorta).
statement: Arteri/o belongs to the cardiovascular system.
wowFactor: Arteries carry oxygenated blood away from the heart — arteri/o comes from the Greek word for airway, because ancient physicians thought arteries carried air!

variants.question: Which combining form refers to an artery?
Arteriosclerosis is a hardening of the walls of which body system?

visualDescription: Root ARTERI/O with an arrow pointing to an artery cross-section

quizQuestion: Which body system does pulmon/o belong to?
explanation: Pulmon/o (lung) belongs to the respiratory system. Other respiratory roots include bronch/o (bronchial tubes), trache/o (trachea), rhin/o (nose), and thorac/o (chest/thorax).
statement: Pulmon/o belongs to the respiratory system.
wowFactor: Pulmon/o comes from the Latin 'pulmo' for lung — giving us 'pulmonary' medicine, meaning anything related to the lungs and breathing!

variants.question: Name a combining form from the respiratory system.
Pulmonary embolism is a blockage affecting which body system?

visualDescription: Root PULMON/O with an arrow pointing to a lung diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does bronch/o belong to?
explanation: Bronch/o (bronchial tubes) belongs to the respiratory system. Other respiratory roots include pulmon/o (lung), trache/o (trachea), rhin/o (nose), and laryng/o (larynx).
statement: Bronch/o belongs to the respiratory system.
wowFactor: The bronchi are the branching airways inside the lungs — bronchoscopy lets doctors look directly into these airways with a tiny camera!

variants.question: Which combining form refers to the bronchial tubes?
Bronchitis is inflammation within which body system?

visualDescription: Root BRONCH/O with an arrow pointing to bronchial tube diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does gastr/o belong to?
explanation: Gastr/o (stomach) belongs to the digestive system. Other digestive roots include hepat/o (liver), enter/o (intestines), col/o (colon), and esophag/o (esophagus).
statement: Gastr/o belongs to the digestive system.
wowFactor: Gastr/o appears in 'gastronomy' (the art of cooking) AND medicine — proving that whether you're a chef or a doctor, it all comes back to the stomach!

variants.question: Name a combining form from the digestive system.
Gastroenteritis is inflammation affecting which body system?

visualDescription: Root GASTR/O with an arrow pointing to a stomach diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does hepat/o belong to?
explanation: Hepat/o (liver) belongs to the digestive system. The liver is the largest digestive gland. Other digestive roots include gastr/o (stomach), enter/o (intestines), pancreat/o (pancreas), and col/o (colon).
statement: Hepat/o belongs to the digestive system.
wowFactor: The liver performs over 500 distinct functions — hepat/o is one of the busiest roots in medicine, appearing in hepatitis, hepatomegaly, and hepatocellular carcinoma!

variants.question: Which combining form refers to the liver?
Hepatitis is inflammation of an organ in which body system?

visualDescription: Root HEPAT/O with an arrow pointing to a liver diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does nephr/o belong to?
explanation: Nephr/o (kidney) belongs to the urinary system. Other urinary roots include cyst/o (bladder), ureter/o (ureter), urethr/o (urethra), and pyel/o (renal pelvis).
statement: Nephr/o belongs to the urinary system.
wowFactor: Nephr/o comes from the Greek 'nephros' — the same root behind nephrite jade, which ancient peoples believed could cure kidney disease when worn or ingested!

variants.question: Name a combining form from the urinary system.
Nephrology is the medical specialty focused on which body system?

visualDescription: Root NEPHR/O with an arrow pointing to a kidney diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does cyst/o belong to?
explanation: Cyst/o (bladder) belongs to the urinary system. Other urinary roots include nephr/o (kidney), ureter/o (ureter), urethr/o (urethra), and ren/o (kidney, Latin root).
statement: Cyst/o belongs to the urinary system.
wowFactor: Cyst/o technically means 'sac' or 'bag' and originally referred to the bladder — a perfect name for an organ that's essentially a muscular storage sac for urine!

variants.question: Which combining form refers to the urinary bladder?
Cystitis is inflammation of an organ in which body system?

visualDescription: Root CYST/O with an arrow pointing to a urinary bladder diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does neur/o belong to?
explanation: Neur/o (nerve) belongs to the nervous system. Other nervous system roots include encephal/o (brain), myel/o (spinal cord), cerebr/o (cerebrum), and gangli/o (ganglion).
statement: Neur/o belongs to the nervous system.
wowFactor: The human nervous system has roughly 86 billion neurons — neur/o is Greek for 'sinew' or 'nerve', and ancient physicians used it for both tendons and nerves since they looked similar!

variants.question: Name a combining form from the nervous system.
Neurology is the medical specialty focused on which body system?

visualDescription: Root NEUR/O with an arrow pointing to a nerve cell diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does encephal/o belong to?
explanation: Encephal/o (brain) belongs to the nervous system. Other nervous system roots include neur/o (nerve), myel/o (spinal cord), cerebr/o (cerebrum), and mening/o (meninges).
statement: Encephal/o belongs to the nervous system.
wowFactor: Encephalitis (inflammation of the brain) and encephalogram (brain scan) both come from encephal/o — Greek for 'within the head', literally describing the brain's location!

variants.question: Which combining form refers to the brain?
An electroencephalogram (EEG) measures electrical activity in which body system?

visualDescription: Root ENCEPHAL/O with an arrow pointing to a brain diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does oste/o belong to?
explanation: Oste/o (bone) belongs to the musculoskeletal system. Other musculoskeletal roots include arthr/o (joint), my/o (muscle), tend/o (tendon), and chondr/o (cartilage).
statement: Oste/o belongs to the musculoskeletal system.
wowFactor: Oste/o comes from Greek 'osteon' for bone — osteoporosis literally means 'porous bones', vividly describing how this condition makes bones fragile and full of holes!

variants.question: Name a combining form from the musculoskeletal system.
Osteoporosis is a disease affecting which body system?

visualDescription: Root OSTE/O with an arrow pointing to a bone cross-section diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does arthr/o belong to?
explanation: Arthr/o (joint) belongs to the musculoskeletal system. Other musculoskeletal roots include oste/o (bone), my/o (muscle), chondr/o (cartilage), and ligament/o (ligament).
statement: Arthr/o belongs to the musculoskeletal system.
wowFactor: Arthr/o is easily confused with arteri/o (artery) — arthroscopy examines joints, while arteriosclerosis affects blood vessels. One extra letter makes a big difference!

variants.question: Which combining form refers to a joint?
Arthritis is inflammation of structures in which body system?

visualDescription: Root ARTHR/O with an arrow pointing to a joint diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does derm/o belong to?
explanation: Derm/o (skin) belongs to the integumentary system. Other integumentary roots include cutane/o (skin), onych/o (nail), trich/o (hair), and hidr/o (sweat).
statement: Derm/o belongs to the integumentary system.
wowFactor: The integumentary system is the largest organ system by surface area — derm/o comes from Greek 'derma', giving us dermatology, epidermis, and even 'taxidermy' (arrangement of skin)!

variants.question: Name a combining form from the integumentary system.
Dermatitis is inflammation within which body system?

visualDescription: Root DERM/O with an arrow pointing to a skin layer diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does thyr/o belong to?
explanation: Thyr/o (thyroid gland) belongs to the endocrine system. Other endocrine roots include aden/o (gland), adren/o (adrenal gland), pancreat/o (pancreas), and gonad/o (gonads).
statement: Thyr/o belongs to the endocrine system.
wowFactor: Thyr/o comes from Greek 'thyreos' meaning shield — the thyroid gland was named for its shield shape! It controls metabolism, growth, and body temperature through hormone secretion.

variants.question: Name a combining form from the endocrine system.
Hypothyroidism is a disorder of which body system?

visualDescription: Root THYR/O with an arrow pointing to a thyroid gland diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does aden/o belong to?
explanation: Aden/o (gland) belongs to the endocrine system. Other endocrine roots include thyr/o (thyroid), adren/o (adrenal gland), pancreat/o (pancreas, as an endocrine organ), and hypophys/o (pituitary).
statement: Aden/o belongs to the endocrine system.
wowFactor: Aden/o means 'gland' and is used for both endocrine and exocrine glands — but in system classification, it is associated with the endocrine system since glands are the defining organ of that system!

variants.question: Which combining form refers to a gland?
Adenoma (a gland tumor) is most closely associated with which body system?

visualDescription: Root ADEN/O with an arrow pointing to an endocrine gland diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does gynec/o belong to?
explanation: Gynec/o (woman/female) belongs to the reproductive system. Other reproductive roots include orchi/o (testis), hyster/o (uterus), oophor/o (ovary), and colp/o (vagina).
statement: Gynec/o belongs to the reproductive system.
wowFactor: Gynec/o comes from Greek 'gyne' for woman — the same root that gives us 'gynecology', the medical specialty devoted to female reproductive health!

variants.question: Name a combining form from the reproductive system.
A gynecologist specializes in which body system?

visualDescription: Root GYNEC/O with an arrow pointing to a female reproductive anatomy diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does orchi/o belong to?
explanation: Orchi/o (testis) belongs to the reproductive system. Other reproductive roots include gynec/o (female), hyster/o (uterus), prostat/o (prostate), and sperm/o (sperm).
statement: Orchi/o belongs to the reproductive system.
wowFactor: Orchi/o comes from Greek 'orchis' meaning testis — the orchid flower was named after it due to its bulb shape! Orchidectomy (surgical removal) uses this root.

variants.question: Which combining form refers to the testis?
Orchitis is inflammation of an organ in which body system?

visualDescription: Root ORCHI/O with an arrow pointing to a male reproductive anatomy diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does ophthalm/o belong to?
explanation: Ophthalm/o (eye) belongs to the sensory system (eye). Other eye-related roots include ocul/o (eye, Latin), retin/o (retina), ir/o (iris), and conjunctiv/o (conjunctiva).
statement: Ophthalm/o belongs to the sensory system — specifically the eye.
wowFactor: Ophthalm/o is one of the hardest-to-spell roots in medical terminology — ophthalmology has two 'h's and three vowels in a row! Medical students have struggled with this spelling for centuries.

variants.question: Name a combining form from the sensory eye system.
An ophthalmologist specializes in which sense organ?

visualDescription: Root OPHTHALM/O with an arrow pointing to an eye anatomy diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does ot/o belong to?
explanation: Ot/o (ear) belongs to the sensory system (ear). Other ear-related roots include audi/o (hearing), tympan/o (eardrum), myring/o (eardrum), and cochle/o (cochlea).
statement: Ot/o belongs to the sensory system — specifically the ear.
wowFactor: Ot/o appears in 'otolaryngology' (ENT medicine) — the combined specialty of ear (ot/o), nose (rhin/o), and throat (laryng/o). It is one of the longest specialty names in medicine!

variants.question: Which combining form refers to the ear?
Otitis media (middle ear infection) affects which body system?

visualDescription: Root OT/O with an arrow pointing to an ear anatomy diagram

quizQuestion: Which body system does lymph/o belong to?
explanation: Lymph/o (lymph/lymphatic tissue) belongs to the hematologic/lymphatic system. Other hematologic/lymphatic roots include hem/o (blood), splen/o (spleen), thromb/o (clot), and leuk/o (white blood cell).
statement: Lymph/o belongs to the hematologic/lymphatic system.
wowFactor: The lymphatic system has no pump — unlike blood which is pushed by the heart, lymph fluid moves through muscle contractions and breathing. Lymph/o comes from Latin 'lympha' meaning clear water!

variants.question: Name a combining form from the hematologic/lymphatic system.
Lymphoma is a cancer originating in which body system?

visualDescription: Root LYMPH/O with an arrow pointing to a lymphatic system diagram
